summary: Next.js 15 (published 2024-10-21) makes request-time APIs asynchronous and flips core caching defaults. cookies(), headers(), draftMode(), and the params/searchParams props (in layout.js, page.js, route.js, default.js, generateMetadata, generateViewport, and metadata image files) now return Promises and must be awaited (or unwrapped with React use() in client components); synchronous access temporarily works with dev warnings. Caching inverts: fetch() requests, GET Route Handlers, and client-router page segments are no longer cached by default — restore with cache: 'force-cache', dynamic = 'force-static', and experimental.staleTimes respectively. The App Router requires React 19 (Pages Router keeps React 18 compatibility); useFormState is deprecated for useActionState. The external @next/font package is removed in favor of built-in next/font. next.config renames: experimental.bundlePagesExternals -> bundlePagesRouterDependencies, experimental.serverComponentsExternalPackages -> serverExternalPackages. runtime = 'experimental-edge' now errors (use 'edge'). NextRequest geo/ip are removed (use @vercel/functions). next/image drops squoosh (sharp is used automatically), Speed Insights auto-instrumentation is removed, and the minimum Node.js version is 18.18.0. Codemods automate most of it: npx @next/codemod@canary upgrade latest, and next-async-request-api for the async APIs.

breaking_changes.title: cookies(), headers(), draftMode() are now async
breaking_changes.description: The request-time APIs from next/headers return Promises in Next.js 15. Synchronous access still works temporarily (with a dev warning) via the UnsafeUnwrapped* types, but is slated for removal.
breaking_changes.migration_note: Add await at every call site (the enclosing function becomes async). Codemod: npx @next/codemod@canary next-async-request-api . — the escape hatch cookies() as unknown as UnsafeUnwrappedCookies logs warnings and only buys time until the next major.

breaking_changes.title: params and searchParams props are now Promises in pages, layouts, and metadata
breaking_changes.description: params (in layout.js, page.js, default.js, generateMetadata, generateViewport, opengraph-image, twitter-image, icon, apple-icon) and searchParams (in page.js) are Promise-typed. Server components await them; client components unwrap with React's use().
breaking_changes.migration_note: Update BOTH the runtime access (await / use()) and the prop TYPES (Params becomes Promise<{...}>) — type-only fixes that skip the await produce '[object Promise]' bugs. This is the single most common v15 break agents write incorrectly from v14-era training data. Codemod: next-async-request-api.

breaking_changes.title: params in Route Handlers are now a Promise
breaking_changes.description: The second argument of Route Handler methods (GET, POST, …) carries params as a Promise that must be awaited.
breaking_changes.migration_note: Same Promise pattern as pages: update the type to Promise<...> and await it inside the handler.

breaking_changes.title: fetch() requests are no longer cached by default
breaking_changes.description: Server-side fetch() in the App Router defaults to uncached in v15 (v14 cached by default). Additionally, dynamic = 'force-dynamic' now sets a no-store default on the fetch cache.
breaking_changes.migration_note: Silent performance/cost regression rather than a compile error: origin APIs get hit on every request after upgrading. Re-add caching per request (cache: 'force-cache') or per segment (fetchCache = 'default-cache').

breaking_changes.title: GET Route Handlers are no longer cached by default
breaking_changes.description: In v14, GET Route Handlers were cached by default unless they used dynamic functions/config. In v15 they are uncached by default. Metadata routes (sitemap.ts, opengraph-image.tsx, icon.tsx) remain static by default.
breaking_changes.before_code:
// v14: cached by default
export async function GET() {}
breaking_changes.migration_note: Audit GET route handlers that relied on implicit caching (e.g. expensive computations, third-party calls) and add export const dynamic = 'force-static' where the old behavior is wanted.

breaking_changes.title: Client Router Cache: page segments no longer reused on navigation
breaking_changes.description: Page segments get a staleTime of 0: navigating via <Link> or useRouter always fetches fresh page data. Shared layouts and loading.js stay cached (loading.js for 5 minutes), and back/forward navigation still restores from cache.
breaking_changes.before_code: // v14: revisiting a page within 30s reused the client cache — no config needed
breaking_changes.migration_note: Apps that felt 'instant' on back-and-forth navigation may issue more server requests after upgrading. experimental.staleTimes restores the old feel; leaving the default guarantees fresh data.

breaking_changes.title: App Router requires React 19
breaking_changes.description: Minimum react/react-dom is 19 for the App Router. The Pages Router retains React 18 backward compatibility (running both React versions in one app is possible but not recommended).
breaking_changes.migration_note: Upgrade React together with Next (npx @next/codemod@canary upgrade latest handles it). useFormState is deprecated in favor of useActionState (see deprecations); useFormStatus gains data/method/action keys on React 19.

breaking_changes.title: @next/font package removed — use built-in next/font
breaking_changes.description: The external @next/font package no longer works; the font utilities are built into next/font. Font-family hashing was also removed.
breaking_changes.migration_note: Rename imports and drop the @next/font dependency. Codemod: built-in-next-font.

breaking_changes.title: next.config: experimental.serverComponentsExternalPackages -> serverExternalPackages
breaking_changes.description: The option is stable and renamed; the experimental key no longer applies.
breaking_changes.migration_note: Move the array to the top level under the new name. v15 also adds next.config.ts support with the NextConfig type if you want typed config while touching this file.

breaking_changes.title: next.config: experimental.bundlePagesExternals -> bundlePagesRouterDependencies
breaking_changes.description: The Pages Router external-bundling option is stable and renamed.
breaking_changes.migration_note: Top-level rename; combine with serverExternalPackages to opt specific packages out of bundling across both routers.

breaking_changes.title: runtime = 'experimental-edge' now errors
breaking_changes.description: The segment config value 'experimental-edge' (same meaning as 'edge') is no longer accepted — builds error until it is renamed.

breaking_changes.title: NextRequest geo and ip properties removed
breaking_changes.description: request.geo and request.ip are gone from NextRequest — those values come from the hosting provider. On Vercel, use geolocation() and ipAddress() from @vercel/functions.
breaking_changes.migration_note: npm install @vercel/functions on Vercel; on other hosts read the equivalent request headers your provider sets. A codemod automates the Vercel variant.

breaking_changes.title: next/image: squoosh removed, sharp used automatically; Content-Disposition now attachment
breaking_changes.description: The squoosh WASM optimizer is removed and sharp becomes the (auto-installed at runtime) optimizer for next start and standalone output. The optimized-image Content-Disposition default changed to attachment, and src values with leading/trailing spaces now error.
breaking_changes.before_code:
// v14 self-hosting: sharp was a manual recommendation
// npm install sharp  (falls back to squoosh if missing)
breaking_changes.after_code:
// v15: no manual step — sharp is used automatically with
// `next start` or standalone output mode
breaking_changes.migration_note: Self-hosted deployments no longer silently fall back to slow WASM optimization. Watch the Content-Disposition change if images were expected to render inline when opened directly, and trim whitespace in src values.

breaking_changes.title: Speed Insights auto-instrumentation removed
breaking_changes.description: Next.js no longer auto-instruments Vercel Speed Insights; the dedicated @vercel/speed-insights package is required.
breaking_changes.migration_note: Follow the Vercel Speed Insights quickstart — metrics silently stop flowing after the upgrade otherwise.

breaking_changes.title: Minimum Node.js version is 18.18.0
breaking_changes.description: Next.js 15 raises the minimum required Node.js version to 18.18.0.
breaking_changes.migration_note: Update CI images, Dockerfiles, and engines fields; older 18.x patch lines fail.

breaking_changes.title: revalidateTag/revalidatePath during render now throw
breaking_changes.description: Calling revalidateTag() or revalidatePath() while a component is rendering is an error in v15; they belong in Server Actions or Route Handlers.
breaking_changes.before_code:
// v14: calling revalidateTag('posts') inside a rendering
// Server Component happened to work
breaking_changes.migration_note: Move revalidation calls out of render paths into the mutation that triggers them.

breaking_changes.title: next/dynamic: suspense prop removed; ssr:false disallowed in Server Components
breaking_changes.description: The deprecated suspense prop is removed (no empty Suspense boundary is inserted in the App Router), and using next/dynamic with ssr: false inside a Server Component is now an error.
breaking_changes.before_code:
// In a Server Component (v14 tolerated this):
const Widget = dynamic(() => import('./widget'), { ssr: false })
breaking_changes.migration_note: Wrap client-only dynamic imports in a 'use client' component and render that from the server tree.

deprecations.replacement: await the API (server) or unwrap with React use() (client); UnsafeUnwrappedCookies/UnsafeUnwrappedHeaders/UnsafeUnwrappedDraftMode type the interim
deprecations.removal_timeline: Temporary compatibility only — warns in development and production until the next major version, where synchronous access is removed.
deprecations.note: The codemod (next-async-request-api) converts most call sites; the UnsafeUnwrapped* casts exist to unblock incremental migration, not as a destination.

deprecations.removal_timeline: Still available in React 19 but deprecated; will be removed in a future React release.
deprecations.note: useActionState adds direct access to the pending state. useFormStatus additionally gains data, method, and action keys on React 19.

compatible_with.note: App Router minimum. The Pages Router retains React 18 backward compatibility.
App Router minimum. The Pages Router retains React 18 backward compatibility.
